exit 1; tests 7; pass 1; fail 6; cancelled 0; skipped 0; todo 0. Passing: logs project:task continues to read existing managed log content. Expected RED failures: status --all reports disappeared owned run as ready instead of stale; reconcile command returns unknown_command; events command returns unknown_command; logs --follow --json exits before JSONL because follow is unsupported; clean --safe removes protected running/latest-failed log evidence; logs --follow --json redaction test exits before JSONL because follow is unsupported.

concerns: The stale-state test kills only the fixture-owned managed pid created by launchdeck start, then expects status --all to keep the run record and mark it stale with inspect/reconcile next actions.
The follow tests spawn a bounded launchdeck CLI follower process and terminate only that test-owned follower in cleanup.

Retention coverage configures .launchdeck/logs as a safe clean target to prove clean retention policy protects current running logs, latest failed logs, and failure-linked events even when a broad configured hygiene target overlaps logs.
recovery_hints: Implement reconcile as a safe state-refresh command that updates dead owned runs to stale or stopped, writes events, and does not kill external or unknown listeners.
Add an events CLI command that reads bounded JSONL history by target and returns the standard JSON envelope; --json --follow should stream JSON Lines.
Replace logs --follow unsupported_option with visible follow streaming; with --json, emit one JSON object per line containing schemaVersion, type, runId, line, timestamp, projectId, and task.
Apply redaction to log/event streaming output before writing JSONL to stdout.
Teach clean retention to preserve active run logs, latest failed run logs, and events needed to explain recent failures before removing configured generated targets.
